title: eriogonum heermannii var. floccosum
intro: Eriogonum heermannii var. floccosum, also known as Heermann's buckwheat clay or woolly buckwheat clay, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum heermannii var. floccosum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, with the roots and stems typically being used for medicinal purposes.
